---
title: Muse Component Library
kind: styleguide
slug: component-library-showcase
---

<PlanSummary id="summary" status="Live style guide" title="Component library and real-world review surface">
A polished component inventory for the Muse interactive plan system, shown through a realistic launch-readiness review for a local-first AI feature.
</PlanSummary>

<StatusDashboard id="status" title="Launch readiness dashboard">
Review coverage | 18/18 | All component families visible
Open questions | 3 | Persisted through the local bridge
Risk posture | Medium | One rollout dependency remains
Approval state | Draft | Ready for reviewer interaction
</StatusDashboard>

<Callout id="design-principle" title="Design principle" tone="decision">
This library stays vanilla HTML, CSS, and TypeScript. It does not introduce React, Agent Native, or a hosted component runtime.
</Callout>

<DecisionMatrix id="decisions" title="Design decisions">
Use renderer-owned components | Keeps command output portable across harnesses | accepted
Add theme toggle in shell | Lets reviewers inspect light and dark modes directly | accepted
Render Mermaid in client runtime | Fixes diagrams without requiring author-side SVG exports | accepted
Keep style guide as fixture | Makes visual regressions reproducible in tests | accepted
</DecisionMatrix>

<ArchitectureDiagram id="flow" title="Live review flow">
flowchart TD
  A["Agent writes plan.mdx"] --> B["Muse renderer"]
  B --> C["Component library shell"]
  C --> D{"Reviewer decision"}
  D -->|"Approve"| E["agent-handoff.json"]
  D -->|"Needs revision"| F["plan-state.json"]
  C --> G["Static export"]
</ArchitectureDiagram>

<ImplementationTimeline id="timeline" title="Real-world launch sequence">
Author a plan with every review component represented
Render the local review page and static export
Collect reviewer answers, checklist state, and comments
Approve the plan and hand off machine-readable implementation context
</ImplementationTimeline>

<RiskRegister id="risks" title="Rollout risks">
Mermaid CDN unavailable | Show readable source fallback and render state marker | medium
Reviewer misses dark-mode issue | Theme toggle is always visible in chrome | low
Plan overfits one workflow | Style guide covers every current MDX component | medium
</RiskRegister>

<FileMap id="file-map" title="Touched files">
plugins/Muse/skills/muse/tools/interactive-plan/render.ts
plugins/Muse/skills/muse/tools/interactive-plan/client.ts
plugins/Muse/skills/muse/tools/interactive-plan/components.ts
plugins/Muse/skills/muse/templates/interactive-plan-shell.html
tests/fixtures/interactive-plans/component-library-showcase/plan.mdx
</FileMap>

<FileTree id="file-tree" title="Generated review packet">
plan.mdx
canvas.mdx
visual-explainer.json
dist/index.html
dist/static-export.html
plan-state.json
agent-handoff.json
</FileTree>

<AnnotatedCode id="code" title="Focused renderer contract" file="tools/interactive-plan/render.ts">
export async function renderPlanFolder(rootDir: string): Promise<{ indexPath: string; staticExportPath: string }> {
  const plan = await loadPlanFolder(rootDir);
  const distDir = join(rootDir, plan.manifest.dist);
  await mkdir(distDir, { recursive: true });
  await writeFile(join(distDir, "index.html"), await renderPlanHtml(plan, false, shell));
  await writeFile(join(distDir, "static-export.html"), await renderPlanHtml(plan, true, shell));
}
</AnnotatedCode>

<DiffTabs id="diffs" title="Example review tabs">
file: render.ts
+ static exports keep diagram rendering
+ interactive exports add persistence handlers
---
file: client.ts
+ theme toggle persists reviewer preference
+ Mermaid diagrams render into SVG canvases
+ diagram controls expose action names and arrow-key panning
---
file: shell.html
+ top-level display controls
+ Mermaid runtime script
</DiffTabs>

<ApiSurface id="api" title="Local bridge API">
Endpoint | Method | Purpose
/api/state | POST | Persist answers, checklist values, and revision status
/api/comments | POST | Add or resolve anchored review comments
/api/approve | POST | Create agent-handoff.json and approve plan
/plan-state.json | GET | Inspect current local review state
</ApiSurface>

<DataModel id="data-model" title="Review state model">
Field | Type | Notes
status | draft / needs_revision / approved | Drives body data-review-status
answers | Record<string, string> | QuestionForm persistence
checklist | Record<string, boolean> | Checklist persistence
unresolvedCommentIds | string[] | Approval-readiness signal
</DataModel>

<BeforeAfter id="before-after" title="Before and after">
A minimal fixture showed one approval button, a basic table, and an unrendered Mermaid source block.
---
The component library shows every MDX component, a full launch-review story, rendered diagrams, persisted controls, and explicit light/dark themes.
</BeforeAfter>

<Wireframe id="wireframe" title="Review app wireframe" surface="browser">
<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1.2fr;gap:16px;padding:18px;min-height:260px">
  <aside style="border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:14px;padding:14px;background:var(--surface-elevated)">
    <p class="wf-muted">Launch dossier</p>
    <h3 style="margin:.25rem 0">AI Review Bridge</h3>
    <button class="primary">Start review</button>
  </aside>
  <section style="border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:14px;padding:14px;background:var(--surface)">
    <p class="wf-muted">Reviewer actions</p>
    <label class="ve-ip-check"><input type="checkbox" checked /> <span>Architecture reviewed</span></label>
    <label class="ve-ip-check"><input type="checkbox" /> <span>Risks accepted</span></label>
  </section>
</div>
</Wireframe>

<StateGallery id="states" title="Interactive states">
<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(180px,1fr));gap:12px;padding:16px">
  <div style="border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:14px;padding:14px;background:var(--surface-elevated)"><strong>Draft</strong><p class="wf-muted">Questions open, checklist empty.</p></div>
  <div style="border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:14px;padding:14px;background:color-mix(in oklch,var(--warn) 12%,var(--surface))"><strong>Needs revision</strong><p class="wf-muted">Reviewer asked for changes.</p></div>
  <div style="border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:14px;padding:14px;background:color-mix(in oklch,var(--ok) 12%,var(--surface))"><strong>Approved</strong><p class="wf-muted">Handoff packet generated.</p></div>
</div>
</StateGallery>

<CommentAnchor id="component-anchor" />

<QuestionForm id="questions" title="Reviewer questions">
palette | Does the terracotta, sage, teal, and gold palette feel like the right Muse direction? | freeform
component-system | Should this remain vanilla, or is a larger component system warranted later? | freeform
launch-risk | What would block this from being the default demo? | freeform
</QuestionForm>

<Checklist id="checks" title="Style guide checks">
all-components | Every supported MDX component is represented
mermaid | Mermaid renders as an SVG with zoom controls
theme-toggle | Light and dark themes can be toggled in the page chrome
live-use-case | The page tells a realistic launch-review story
</Checklist>

<ApprovalGate id="approval" title="Approval">
Approve this style-guide direction when the component inventory, real-world use case, and theme behavior feel ready to become the canonical demo.
</ApprovalGate>
